title: Lockstep meter recon -- pin the meter the official anchor captures

step1_official_tps_definition.tokens_counted: OUTPUT tokens only (prompt excluded; total_tps adds input but is not the headline)
step1_official_tps_definition.special_eos_tokens: ignore_eos=true -> generation never stops at EOS; exactly output_len=512 tokens counted per request (EOS, if emitted, is a normal counted token)
step1_official_tps_definition.estimator: time-weighted total/total (sum of output tokens / single wall-clock duration)
step1_official_tps_definition.window: warm steady-state: WARMUP_REQUESTS=4 sent and DISCARDED before the benchmark timer
step1_official_tps_definition.ppl: separate post-benchmark stage; NOT inside dur_s
step1_official_tps_definition.concurrency: max_concurrency=1 (strictly sequential, one request in flight)

steady.estimator: unweighted arithmetic mean of vLLM per-interval 'Avg generation throughput'
steady.window: ALL logged intervals (cold-start ramp + warm + any post-decode PPL-phase intervals)
steady.source: BASELINE.md PR#43 tps_local_splitkv_steady; retired by lawine #72

wall_tps.window: the 128-prompt decode wall window (cold-start INCLUDED; PPL excluded -- separate stage)

windowed-steady.estimator: unweighted arithmetic mean of vLLM per-interval 'Avg generation throughput'
windowed-steady.window: WARM intervals only (drop first W=3 cold intervals; PPL excluded)

step2_single_run_decomposition.phase_split_marker: Running:0 reqs (no generation request in flight) == PPL stage
step2_single_run_decomposition.note: All terms are DEFINITIONAL (estimator x window). The underlying decode is identical -- wall_tps holds to CV 0.035% across 12 fresh restarts (#72).

step2_spread_attribution.axis: WINDOW (cold-start + PPL-phase), on the fragile unweighted-mean estimator
step2_spread_attribution.split_ratio_source: single committed MBT=512 run (PPL 37.97 : cold 10.89 TPS)

table.mechanism: unweighted mean includes post-decode PPL intervals (282/6.4 tps) that official dur_s excludes (PPL is a separate stage)
unweighted mean over-weights the per-decode CUDA-graph/cache-ramp first interval (~29% below warm); official discards 4 warmup requests

estimator_axis_separate.mechanism: separates wall_tps (total/total = official) from the two interval-mean meters; this is the axis that, once aligned, COLLAPSES the residual

step3_alignment.lockstep_meter_definition: num_completion_tokens / decode_duration_s from official decode_outputs.py (128x512, seed 1, conc=1 via MAX_NUM_SEQS=1, ignore_eos), median of N=3 fresh runs, decode-only (PPL separate), captured AS-IS == cold-start included / no warmup discard (so it stays the EXACT definition the #99 multiplier was fit on)

alignment_per_meter.steady: re-estimate as total/total over the decode window (drop PPL, drop cold, time-weight) -> wall_tps
alignment_per_meter.windowed-steady: re-estimate the warm window as total/total instead of unweighted mean -> wall_tps
alignment_per_meter.wall_tps: ALREADY total/total over the decode window == official output_throughput estimator (no correction)

step3_alignment.residual_basis: wall_tps N=12 fresh-restart RANGE 0.10%% (CV 0.035%%); windowed 0.05%%; steady 0.33%% (#72). After alignment the 7.14%% cross-meter spread is gone -- only the wall_tps physical floor remains, which the official shot itself must absorb.
step3_alignment.matches_rationale: wall_tps is the EXACT official estimator: total output tokens / wall duration == sglang output_throughput = sum(output_lens)/dur_s. The one definitional delta (official's 4-warmup-discard vs local cold-included) is a UNIFORM absolute offset (it shifts all meters equally, not a cross-meter spread) and is already absorbed into the deployed multiplier tau=1.06019 -- so it cancels as long as the lockstep capture uses the SAME cold-included decode_outputs.py definition.

step4_self_consistency_and_protocol.anchor_reproduction_is_necessary_not_suf: one point + one free multiplier fits ANY meter (steady needs x1.124, wall_tps x1.060, windowed x1.047); reproduction does NOT disambiguate -- Step 1 methodology match does
step4_self_consistency_and_protocol.lockstep_capture_command:
.venv/bin/python -m research.tps_noise_floor.run_noise_floor --submission <SPLITK_SUBMISSION> --mode fresh --n-runs 3 --wandb-group lockstep-meter
# -> reports wall_tps = num_completion_tokens / decode_duration_s, median of N=3 (the #72/#82 protocol). Decode-only; PPL runs separately. Capture this number in the SAME job/session as the official HF shot of the SAME submission.
step4_self_consistency_and_protocol.bit_exact_self_check: BIT-EXACT self-check (no new run): the captured lockstep wall_tps, multiplied by the committed deployed multiplier tau=1.0601865 (#99/#116, = official 481.53 / pooled local 454.194), must reproduce the SAME submission's official tps within the residual (wall_tps range 0.10%). Equivalently lop.self_check() recovers 481.53 from the LOCKED linear reference 454.338 to 0.0319% (<= MDE 0.10%). This is NECESSARY not sufficient: ALL THREE meters pass anchor-reproduction trivially on one point -- the DISCRIMINATOR is the Step-1 methodology match, which only wall_tps satisfies.

protocol_patch_for_116.why_cold_included: the #99 multiplier was calibrated as official_warm / local_cold-included; a warm-corrected wall_tps would DOUBLE-COUNT the cold-start (~+1.2%) and bias the banked 2nd pair -- capture RAW decode_outputs.py wall_tps
protocol_patch_for_116.the_one_residual_the_shot_measures: the split-K reduction sync-overhead haircut (#116 tau_eff un-pinnable residual, <=1.26% rel) -- an ABSOLUTE local->official term, NOT a meter-choice spread

reasons: wall_tps methodologically identical to official output_throughput (total/total, output-only); residual after alignment 0.10% <= 1%; reproduces #52 anchor 481.53 -> lockstep meter PINNED = wall_tps.
the 7.14%% spread was PURE definition-mismatch (PPL leak + cold-start on the unweighted-mean estimator); it collapses to the wall_tps floor under the official total/total definition.
